title: festuca indigesta subsp. litardierei
intro: Festuca indigesta subsp. litardierei, also known as Litardiere's fescue, is a type of plant that belongs to the grass family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to aid digestion and reduce inflammation. The plant is said to possess health benefits such as relieving symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and reducing pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Festuca indigesta subsp. litardierei can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's essential oils in topical applications.
